untranslatable_reason: Signal requires market microstructure data (bid/offer levels, time precedence tracking, minimum tick increment pricing, order queue position) that cannot be expressed using standard OHLCV-derived indicators. Concepts like 'improve price by 1 tick', 'existing time-precedence order execution', and 'order fills' are order-management mechanics rather than price-action patterns. The signal is a trading tactic/execution rule, not a technical analysis pattern.
translation_notes: This signal describes an order placement and execution strategy dependent on real-time order book state and exchange-specific tick sizes. It cannot be mechanically backtested using historical price/volume data alone. It would require: (1) live order book snapshots, (2) order ID tracking and time stamps, (3) instrument-specific minimum tick rules, and (4) execution queue simulation—none of which are available in standard backtesting frameworks using only price, volume, and technical indicators.
